project.name: ViewMD
project.description: Native macOS markdown viewer app that opens instantly from Finder with minimal UI and beautiful rendering

goals.primary: Create a macOS application capable of reading markdown files
goals.emphasis: Handle both small and large files efficiently

constraints.performance: Must handle large markdown files without performance issues

technical_implications.architecture: Need streaming or chunked reading for large files
technical_implications.ui_framework: Likely AppKit or SwiftUI for native macOS feel

architect.image_loading_strategy: Placeholder first, lazy-load remote images in background. Only animate GIFs in viewport, pause when scrolled away.
architect.svg_rendering: NSImage native SVG rendering — simplest path, vector-crisp. Ignore embedded JS, render static content only.
architect.image_sizing: Scale to fit content width preserving aspect ratio. Never wider than content area, no horizontal scroll. Use markdown/HTML dimensions if specified.
architect.image_cache: Disk cache with max-size cap and TTL of a few days. Auto-expiring. Separate from app state — more like a browser cache.
architect.animated_gifs: Lazy load, play only when in viewport, pause when scrolled away to preserve near-zero idle CPU.

designer.rendering_clarity: Full markdown rendering - user wants to see formatted output (headings as headings, bold as bold, etc.) NOT raw markdown source. This is a viewer, not an editor.
designer.code_highlighting: Need syntax highlighting for popular languages - can leverage open source solutions like highlight.js or similar fast libraries
designer.visual_approach: Terminal themes will work well with both rendered markdown AND syntax-highlighted code blocks

performance_targets.file_processing: Handle any markdown file size swiftly

technical_approach.markdown_processing: Full CommonMark spec support with syntax highlighting

platform.distribution: Direct download (DMG), code signed and notarized

design.typography: Monospace fonts throughout (SF Mono, Menlo, Fira Code, JetBrains Mono, IBM Plex Mono)
design.themes: Terminal-inspired (Solarized, Monokai, etc.) with light/dark variants
design.appearance: Follows macOS system light/dark mode automatically

components.name: Rendering Engine
components.description: Markdown parsing, AST processing, syntax highlighting, and rendering pipeline

components.name: AppKit UI Layer
components.description: Window management, themes, settings, menus, and accessibility

components.name: macOS Platform Integration
components.description: UTI registration, file handling, system integration, launch optimization
